title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · Graph Retrieval
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a history-forensics evidence packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.
Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
Inspect commits, trees, blobs, tags, and object types without checking out or executing repository content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Log Graph Format evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Log Graph Format assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee3; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Log Graph Format has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Log Graph Format precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee3; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git log lists commits in a universal causal order.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the review Log Graph Format case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the review Log Graph Format case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the review Log Graph Format case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the review Log Graph Format case

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Blame Context, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Blame Context record says a history-forensics evidence packet looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Blame Context: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.
steps.why: Retrieving Blame Context through the concrete a history-forensics evidence packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use blame as a line-origin clue and connect it to surrounding commits, review context, and later movement without assigning fault.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
